## [00:00 - 03:03]

So let's talk about goal setting if you've been through the BPM method part of the training already You will have seen this document at a higher level It's part of the playbook I want to go a bit deeper and kind of talk you to you about the specifics about getting some numbers in place And making sure that you know where you are right now and what you want to get to it may sound like you know one of these Planning things that you really can't be bothered with or you don't believe in but just trust me If you have a baseline now, you know where you are and what you want to get to Makes it so much easier to know if you're on the right path and if you're succeeding or not So the first thing is when i'm doing goal setting i rely on the smart method of goal setting Smart is an acronym as it's described here Your goals should be specific measurable achievable relevant and time based so specific means that it's Clear on exactly what you want to get so if you say i want to make a million dollars It's not really specific if you say In the next 12 months. I want to generate one million dollars of profit for my Business, what are your business name is that specific then it has to be Measurable so it's not no good just saying i want to make a million dollars Is that revenue is that profit etc etc? So, you know in my example, I just said make a million dollars in profit in 12 months time. That's entirely measurable Achievable i think so many people fall over on this one So if i said i'm gonna make a million dollar profit in 12 months But i'm currently generating 10k a month in revenue and barely any profit All of a sudden that's not achievable. So then i would go back to my goal and say right let's make a new specific goal That's more achievable. So let's say i am at 10k a month and i'm not making any profit at the moment I could have a goal to say right i want to grow to 25k a month And generate 5k a month profit in three months time That all of a sudden makes it a bit more achievable and the thing is like when you set some crazy crazy big goals Yes, for some of you it could be super motivational and the thing that gets you out of bed every morning But guess what if it's outreach if you don't believe in it yourself You're not going to have that motivation. So you don't even have to have one goal You could have one big goal that says right, uh, you know within the next two years I want to have a million dollars sitting in my bank account if that's your goal. That's absolutely fine Then you just create smaller Smart goals below that how are you actually going to get there? What are the actual steps? So we talk about being making it achievable. We also talk about making it relevant as well Because the more aligned your goal is to what you want You know for many people it's to have more time freedom It's to have more money in the bank so that you can actually do something with it It could be to make a difference in people's lives. It could be to exit your business It could be to buy other businesses whatever that that is it has to be relevant to you

## [03:03 - 06:05]

So it's no good looking at other people and you know falling into traps of they've won an award And I really want that award. That's not really what you want. It's not relevant to your business It's not relevant to your customers. So really really think about that And then the final one is making sure your goals are time-based. So in my example again I said make a million dollar in profit within the next 12 months So I know whether I've achieved that or not and I can break it down into smaller goals So if it's a million dollars over 12 months, then I can have quarterly goals and divide that by four and say I want 250k in profit In within month within the first three months and another 250k in first six months Or maybe you break it down differently and say right. I want a hundred k profit in the first three months Then I want 300k profit in the next three months after that Etc etc etc So you can also break down your goals to make them easier to achieve because when you start getting your big big goals down into months into weeks Into days Now you can see if you're on track or not because guess what when you're sailing the sea in your Your ship or your boat or whatever it is How do you know that you're getting closer to your destination? Right, so you know where you are You know where you need to get to you can calculate how long it's going to take you to get there You can look at the speed the direction Look at the weather forecast and it's the same in business You can look at all those signals and inputs and data to figure out if you are on track and whether you're Heading to the right direction as well. So just think about what that when you're goal setting Then what I've done is to break this down into six parts that you can specifically write down for your business first part is Think about what shift you want to make from this training Be specific and use measurables For example, I want to increase sales from 100k per month to 250k per month. Now. That's specific But it's not time-based and that's what we come back to this and say Um, you know, how how do I want to actually put a time-based goal against it? So then we might add that and say add to this and say within the next three months and that's totally fine If that's within your capability Even if it's not if even if getting to that 250k mark seems really really difficult right now Having that goal makes it easier to make the right decision. So if you're getting 100k Sales per month right now and you want to get to 250. How do you do that more clients more profit? More retention, whatever it is. You can figure out the path. You need to hire more people. Do you need to outsource more? You can then break that down as well And and you know follow the smart method when you're thinking about the goals Then it's about writing down what the current things are that are holding you back from this goal So let's say for example, you've been stuck at 80k 90k 100k months for the last five six months What's holding you back number one could be more traffic to your funnel

## [06:05 - 09:08]

Number two could be actually getting more phone calls or more conversions or whatever it is Whatever your business is number three could be don't have enough people number four could be our systems are not very good number five could be et cetera Write those down make a reference of those because when you're going through this training They will probably still continue to hold you back But the more you're aware of them the better you can find a way to overcome them So for example, if you're on the the the supported program and you turn up to q&a calls You can ask those if you're in the facebook group You can network and ask other people of those but be consciously aware of the things that are currently holding you back Part of it could be I don't have the systems to make meta ads work And that's totally fine as well Obviously through through this program you should be able to solve that one But have those listed up then think about your strengths and weaknesses with Facebook and you know meta ads Becoming aware will help you through the program So think about strengths for some of you You have strengths either personally or in your business for creativity for planning for strategy for big box thinking Maybe it's for attracting clients. Maybe you're so good at the front end of your business Maybe your weakness is actually getting your clients results And retaining their business. Maybe you're running the e-com store and your strengths are You have an amazing products. You've got some great reviews.
